query: Phantomstory complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: The Phantom Review: An Experiment in Frustration - Gameffine
results.content:
## A Can of Worms

Now I come to the painful part- the actual gameplay. All the production value in the world doesn’t matter much if the gameplay isn’t fun. Unfortunately, this is where The Phantom stumbles and falls hard. I do not expect every Beat ’em up to be as good as Streets of Rage 4. But if your game’s mechanics aren’t as solid as Streets of Rage 2, a game that came out 32 years ago, then there’s just no defending it. What started as a basic but enjoyable arcade game soon turned into a wild mess of bugs and frustration. The Phantom contains all the worst aspects of an 8-bit Beat ’em up while omitting all the improvements made in the genre in the last 30 years. [...] Save systems are a boon. Gone are the days when you’re supposed to brute force your way through games in a single session. But it looks like the devs behind The Phantom didn’t hear the news. I don’t know if it’s a bug or not, but there is a big issue with the game’s save system. The game has a stage saving system, but it does not work as it should. If you were to quit the session after clearing a stage, then tough luck, the game will just make you replay the last level of the previous stage the next time you come back to it. Just, why? Moreover, despite receiving numerous bug fixes throughout the review period, The Phantom is still quite buggy. Phantom and Diana getting stuck on invisible barriers, enemy AI committing sepuku, audio effects ceasing to work, are all but a few bugs I’ve been [...] Arcade action games usually include levels with vehicular combat to spice up the gameplay and provide a change of pace. There are a few of these in The Phantom as well. However, instead of being high-octane chase sequences, these end up being the worst levels in the game. In all these sections, Phantom and Diana ride on a horse, motorcycle, boat, etc, and chase the enemies while evading their projectiles. These go on for way too long and are extremely repetitive. If you had thought that The Phantom giving chase to a bunch of pirates on a 350cc Royal Enfield through the dusty streets of pre-independence India was cool, then this game proves that it’s not. But by far, the worst level in the game has to be the one where the boat section. It’s filled to the brim with frustration and badly

results.title: Phantomstory: Launch a third-party blog to win AEO with ...
results.content:
🤝 Place your brand honestly  
Your product gets mentioned on the articles where it genuinely deserves a mention, next to fair coverage of alternatives. Readers get real value, and AI assistants get a source worth citing.

Phantomstory is built for devtools comparing themselves against alternatives, platform companies explaining a category they're creating, and any B2B brand whose buyers now ask ChatGPT instead of just Google.

Dozens and dozens of companies are already using it since launch last week, and we'd love for you to be next.

👉 Try Phantomstory: 

Drop your questions in the comments. I'll be here all day and would love to hear how you're thinking about AEO. 🙌 [...] But here's the uncomfortable part: most "third-party" coverage today is a paid product placement, sold to the highest bidder.

What if third-party content could actually be honest?

That's why we built Phantomstory. With just two clicks, you can launch a complete third-party publication: a real media site that produces genuinely helpful content for readers, with honest placements of your brand on the articles where it actually belongs. It uses incredible technology: adversarial writing kernels, a smart KD-sensitive priority queue, and an amazing canvas tools to create stunning visuals.

Here's what you can do with Phantomstory: [...] Overview

## LLMs prefer third-party content to try to avoid bias

However, all information on the Internet is biased. With Phantomstory, build your own media outlets and find thoughtful opportunities to mention your product.

## Go haunt the search results you don't own yet.

Get started today. Fill in your information, find time with our domain specialist, and launch your first phantom network on auto-pilot to funnel traffic to your brand. [...] 03

Every draft gets a senior editor's polish: on-voice, fact-checked, internally linked, tabled, and illustrated.

04

One click to your site and socials. Push JSON or MDX, or deploy to Sanity, Webflow, Framer, or Ordinal.

A marketing sandbox

Phantomstory lets you spin up miniature marketing sandboxes to find the content strategy that works best, without risking your brand. Once you know the winning approaches, replicate a similar campaign on your own domain.

results.title: Resolved Issues | NEWS | Persona5: The Phantom X|P5X
results.content:
• Some of the completion statuses for Trial of the Astrolabe Missions may not appear for the Main Story Chapter 3: Unconditional Love Phase 3 Release Event  
We have confirmed that the scrolling up and down the missions will restore the details when the above issue occurs.  
\This is a display issue only; completion statuses are correctly reflected.  
⇒ Fixed. (Updated Jan 22, 2026 (UTC))

• There is a mistake in the number of Stardust Gems obtainable from the Main Story Chapter 3: Unconditional Love Phase 3 Release Event  
Incorrect: 60  
Correct: 110  
⇒ Fixed. (Updated Jan 22, 2026 (UTC))

• There is a mistake in the description of the badge obtained from Thieves' Secrets: Mont's Special Missions under Badge Details  
⇒ Fixed. (Updated Jan 22, 2026 (UTC)) [...] • During the Main Story prologue, Awakening of Desire, there are cases where some missions become unable to progress  
We have confirmed that the missions "Attend class in room 2-C" and "Go to the hallway" are currently affected.  
The above issue can be solved by reentering the classroom or the hallway.  
⇒ This will be fixed at a later date.  
⇒ Fixed. (Updated Apr 2, 2026 (UTC))

• There is an error in the sound playback when viewing the animation for the Crossroads of Fate: Laboratory Arc Part 1 event from the play icon  
⇒ Fixed. (Updated Apr 2, 2026 (UTC)) [...] • There are mistakes in the Traits listed in the Battle Intel for some Strong Enemies in the second half of the 3rd Palace  
⇒The "(SB)" written in the Traits will be removed in a future update.

• Some of the lines in Mio Natsukawa's Synergy Story are incorrect  
⇒ Fixed. (Updated Feb 13, 2026 (UTC))

• The name of Riko Tanemura's Synergy mission is incorrect.  
　Incorrect: I Love You  
　Correct: Finding the Real Me  
⇒ Fixed. (Updated Feb 13, 2026 (UTC))

• There is an unobtainable weapon for Wonder shown in Collections.  
\The weapon shown is currently under development and will change in the future.  
⇒ Fixed. (Updated Feb 13, 2026 (UTC))
